name: v05-hello-global-mw
description: Demo of v0.5.4 process-global middleware. The workflow itself declares NO middleware — neither at the workflow root nor on the trigger. Yet every request to this endpoint runs through whatever middleware the operator registered globally via WorkflowRegistry.setGlobalMiddleware([...]) at boot, or via the BLOK_GLOBAL_MIDDLEWARE env var (comma-separated names) when that fallback path is used. Demonstrates that ops middleware (request-id, audit-log, OpenTelemetry spans) can wrap every workflow uniformly without touching individual workflow files. To exercise: BLOK_GLOBAL_MIDDLEWARE=auth-check bun run http:dev, then POST without auth → 401, POST with matching Authorization + X-Expected-Token → 200.

inputs.expression: ({ greeting: 'hello, world', identity: ctx.state.identity || null, ts: Date.now() })
